author: śrīla saccidānanda bhaktibinoda ṭhākura
verses:
hari he!
āmi ta’ cañcalamati, amaryāda, kṣudra ati,
asūyā⋅prasava sadā mora
pāpiṣṭha, kṛtaghna, mānī, nṛśaṁsa, vañcane jñānī,
kāmavaśe thāki’ sadā ghora

e hena durjana ha’ye, e duḥkha⋅jaladhi va’ye,
caritechi saṁsāra⋅sāgare
kemane e bhavāmvudhi, pāra ha’ye niravadhi,
tava pādasevā mile more

tomāra karuṇā pāi, tabe ta’ tariyā jāi,
āmi ei duranta sāgara
tumi prabhu, śrī⋅caraṇe, rākha dāse dhūlisane,
nahe bhaktivinoda kātara
en_translation:
O Hari! Indeed, I am fickle-minded, disrespectful, very insignificant, and my heart always gives birth to envy. I am sinful, ungrateful, proud, cruel, expert in deception, and always deeply immersed in lust.

Having become such a wicked person, bearing this ocean of sorrow, I am wandering in the ocean of material existence. How can I cross this ocean of material existence and incessantly attain service to Your lotus feet?

If I receive Your mercy, then indeed I can cross this formidable ocean. You, my Lord, please keep this servant with the dust at Your beautiful feet; otherwise Bhaktivinoda will be distressed.
